name: Command
summary: The Command class is the preferred class for adding tools to the menus and Ruby toolbars. For example, you could add a menu item and pass it a code block directly, or you could first create a Command. Using Commands gives you greater control over how the item works in the UI, and it allows multiple spots in the UI to call the same code. For example, You might want a toolbar button and a context-click menu item to both point to the same command, and to control the tooltip and its “graying” from a single spot in your code.

constructors.name: new
constructors.summary: Note: Prior to SketchUp 2019 it was not possible to sub-class UI::Command due to a bug in how SketchUp initialized the class. The new method is used to create a new command.
constructors.remarks: Note: Prior to SketchUp 2019 it was not possible to sub-class UI::Command due to a bug in how SketchUp initialized the class. The new method is used to create a new command.

params.name: menutext
params.doc: The text that will appear for this command's menu item if it appears on a menu.

methods.name: get_validation_proc
methods.summary: The #get_validation_proc method returns the command's validation proc.
methods.remarks: The #get_validation_proc method returns the command's validation proc.

methods.name: proc
methods.summary: The #proc method returns the command's proc that is called when the command is invoked.
methods.remarks: The #proc method returns the command's proc that is called when the command is invoked.

methods.name: set_validation_proc
methods.summary: Note: Avoid disabling an command as it often isn't obvious to the user why it is disabled.
methods.remarks: Note: Avoid disabling an command as it often isn't obvious to the user why it is disabled. Prefer keeping the command enabled but show an error message if pressed when it cannot be used. The #set_validation_proc method allows you to change whether the command is enabled, checked, etc. For instance, the command toggling a dialog window may be displayed as checked while the dialog is open.

properties.name: extension
properties.summary: Note: This is an advanced feature that extension developers normally won't have to deal with.
properties.remarks: Note: This is an advanced feature that extension developers normally won't have to deal with. It's purpose is to address scenarios when SketchUp isn't able to automatically infer which extension the command belongs to. The #extension method returns the command's associated extension.

properties.name: large_icon
properties.summary: The large_icon method returns the icon file for the command's large icon.
properties.remarks: The large_icon method returns the icon file for the command's large icon.

properties.name: menu_text
properties.summary: The menu_text method returns the menu item name for the command.
properties.remarks: The menu_text method returns the menu item name for the command.

properties.name: small_icon
properties.summary: The small_icon method returns the icon file for the command's small icon.
properties.remarks: The small_icon method returns the icon file for the command's small icon.

properties.name: status_bar_text
properties.summary: The status_bar_text method returns the status bar text for the command.
properties.remarks: The status_bar_text method returns the status bar text for the command.

properties.name: tooltip
properties.summary: The tooltip method returns command item's tooltip text.
properties.remarks: The tooltip method returns command item's tooltip text.
